plan: Reduce SPY to 20% to meet concentration limits and lower overall market exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Increase QQQI to 25% for greater income and portfolio stability.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Buy TSLA at 5% to diversify with a high-scoring long setup.
This plan aims for capital preservation and lower overall portfolio volatility.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets approximately 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown risk. Returns will be modest, but the portfolio will be more resilient to market downturns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and rebalance.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETF risk and exit positions below stop.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income component.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 15%, for exposure to a quality tech name.
Buy TSLA at 8% to diversify with a strong scanner setup.
This plan balances risk and growth, maintaining a moderate cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets approximately 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while still allowing for growth through quality tech and strong scanner setups. Expect moderate swings, with reduced tail risk compared to current.

moves.note: High R scanner setup, new tech

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and free up capital.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to exit positions below stop and reallocate capital to stronger setups.
Add to CRWD, increasing to 18%, pressing on a working high-score tech name.
Buy TSLA at 12% as the strongest scanner setup with high conviction.
Buy NVDU at 10% to add a high R-value scanner setup in new tech.
Increase SMH to 15%, leaning into the overall semiconductor strength. This plan aims for aggressive growth by concentrating in high-scoring setups and reallocating from underperforming assets.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets approximately 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It concentrates in the highest-scoring scanner setups and reallocates capital from underperforming assets. This approach significantly increases both potential upside and drawdown risk, and requires strict adherence to stops due to amplified moves and potential for rapid losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce broad market exposure for stability.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF risk completely.
Increase QQQI to 30% to maximize the income-focused ETF allocation.
Trim CRWD to 5% to significantly reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift away from growth-oriented index exposure.
This plan prioritizes steady monthly distributions and low drawdown over growth.
income.read: This income plan targets approximately 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It heavily favors income-generating ETFs and significantly reduces exposure to high-beta and leveraged assets. While aiming for steady distributions, NAV can still fall in selloffs, and distributions are variable, not guaranteed.
